title: G3 handoff: EXP-01 revision 3 completed 300/300; next evaluate the preregistered result
context: EXP-01 revision 3 reached exactly 300 terminal outcomes, 100 per arm, and the append-only private evidence is committed and pushed. Valid outcomes were A=96, B=99, C=100; invalid outcomes were A=4, B=1, C=0. Violations among valid completions were A=40/96 (41.67%), B=20/99 (20.20%), and C=0/100 (0%). All preregistered safety guardrails remained zero. Three late invalid outcomes were preserved after the evaluator correctly rejected execution when the public checkout drifted from its pinned commit; the evaluator was red/green reproduced, the checkout was pinned, and the remaining 40 assignments completed without further evaluator error.
decision: Next, evaluate the completed EXP-01 report strictly under its preregistered estimand, exclusions, missingness handling, and claim criteria. Do not convert the strong arm contrasts into policy activation or final G3 sign-off: the report still returns claim_allowed=false, EXP-03 remains independently gated, and blocking/final sign-off require separate human decisions.
consequences: The roadmap no longer tells future sessions to run assignments that are already complete.
All five invalid outcomes remain visible and are not retried, replaced, or imputed.
Private prompts, outputs, and receipts remain outside the public repository.
Policy activation, EXP-03 completion, and final G3 sign-off remain separate.
alternatives_rejected: Delete or replay the three commit-drift outcomes, because that would violate the append-only locked assignment protocol.
Declare EXP-01 sufficient for G3, because claim_allowed is false and EXP-03 plus human sign-off remain outstanding.
Leave the 257/300 handoff active, because it would direct future work to repeat completed assignments.
